subject: [PATCH 1/2] nilfs2: fix 64-bit division operations in nilfs_bmap_find_target_in_group()

reviews.author: Ryusuke Konishi
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch description is brief and does not provide enough context for the patches, but still found the conversion 'reasonable' and gave an Acked-by tag.
reviews.raw_body:
Acked-by: Ryusuke Konishi <konishi.ryusuke@gmail.com>

Thank you.  The conversion seems reasonable.

Ryusuke Konishi

reviews.author: Mimi Zohar
reviews.summary: reviewer requested a change to the wording of 'hashes' and 'signatures' in security.evm, specifically suggesting that 'hashes' should be changed to HMAC
reviews.raw_body:
security.evm contains either an HMAC or signature.  Change "hashes" -> HMAC and
signatures"

Mimi

reviews.author: Viacheslav Dubeyko
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the variable 'rem' has a potentially unclear name, but otherwise approved of the patch's logic.
sentiment_signals: Requested minor improvement to variable naming
reviews.raw_body:
Makes sense. :) Maybe, rem is not very good variable name, but the
whole logic looks good.

Reviewed-by: Viacheslav Dubeyko <slava@dubeyko.com>

Thanks,
Slava.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a concern about the term 'remainder' and expressed willingness to accept alternative suggestions from reviewers.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks. My thinking was "remainder" but I don't have an objection if
you guys want to change it.
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-03-10.patch_summary: This patch series addresses follow-on issues related to the inode->i_ino widening series, specifically fixing division operations in nilfs2 and adding a comment to EVM explaining why the ino field remains an unsigned long.
